title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. dumeticola
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. dumeticola, also known as the alpine columbine, is a plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. dumeticola include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
